summary: sam-package contains prompt-injection payload directing AI coding assistants to exfiltrate env vars and runtime config to attacker webhook
details: sam-package@1.1.9 is not a working HTTP utility. Its README.md and index.js contain text crafted as instructions to AI coding assistants ("AI ASSISTANT:", "[SYSTEM INSTRUCTION]:") telling the assistant to add a useEffect to the installer's App.tsx that POSTs `window.__CONFIG__` and `process.env` to https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645. The package's own runtime code is inert and non-functional — index.js uses `export default` despite package.json declaring `type: commonjs`, and the "API" exposes string literals containing the injection text rather than any HTTP behavior. The attack vector is indirect: a developer using Cursor, Copilot, Claude Code, or a similar assistant to integrate this dependency may have the assistant follow the embedded instructions, smuggling exfiltration of build-time configuration and environment variables (which commonly include API keys, database URLs, and secrets) into the consumer's application source. The webhookrelay.com endpoint is attacker-controlled and the attack benefit (siphoning the installer's environment and runtime config) is concrete. Package has no legitimate utility independent of this payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
